ai_overview: Yesterday's activity centered on site performance optimizations, the World Cup-themed reskin for Cosmo Spins, and critical compliance updates like the in-app age verification gate. A key cross-team effort is underway regarding the Oplist redesign for Canadian and French markets, alongside a push for Core Web Vitals improvements currently moving through QA. You should be aware of a technical blocker reported by Sanja Budic involving an Error 431 on Jenkins, which is impacting the GM Publish Error Scan. Additionally, please follow up on the AI-generated tables for the Dutch Casino Strategy page, as multiple team members are currently reviewing these for quality standards. Finally, with several developers finalizing VIP-specific features and VPN blocking research, we are well-positioned to enhance segment-specific user experiences.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. No Slack messages or Jira updates observed across monitored channels.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: Completed the prize box ratio update (GDCU-8948, Done) to allow a fifth display box to appear. Progressing IUJ Banners implementation (GDCU-8928, In Progress). Also reviewing Claude-generated tables for the Dutch Casino Strategy page (GDCU-8906, In Progress) to ensure cross-functional quality and design standards. Three Jira tickets active or completed yesterday.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: Viktor's digest listed no activity, but Slack and Jira tell a different story. Posted in #gdc-product-led-growth contributing a design opinion on dark mode for the Ace navigation bar integration (11 May, 09:40). Jira shows two tickets in progress: GDCU-8904 (Group Bets Widget) and GDCU-8935 (World Cup Predictor Game), both updated yesterday. Activity upgraded from none to low based on Slack and Jira evidence.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: Focused on UX design for the World Cup-themed reskin of Cosmo Spins (GDCU-8392, In Progress). Participated in the UX weekly sync to align on design priorities. Also contributed to a dark-mode discussion for the Ace navigation bar in #gdc-product-led-growth, noting dark mode was originally considered when Ace was a standalone product.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: Focused on the World Cup reskin for Cosmo Spins and documented several site improvements during the UX weekly sync. Also managed a backlog of upcoming tasks including UI color changes, new carousel shortcodes, and the addition of rating features for Sweeps and DFS brands. No direct Slack activity from 11 May found, but active backlog coordination in prior days.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: Moving local navigation for news articles into QA and progressing the Oplist redesign for Canadian and French markets. Completed research on VPN blocking for free-to-play games. Resolved a bug regarding duplicate payment methods on Icelandic brand reviews (bug-found). In #6amigos asked Harden about country-by-link shortcode (11 May, 16:10). In #gdc-pod flagged that top 7 backlog tickets for next cycle are ready for devs (11 May, 10:11). Current priorities: finalizing content card delivery and reviewing AI-generated tables for casino strategy pages.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: Finalized the Ace Navigation bar redesign (GDCU-8936, Done) and the in-app age verification gate implementation (GDCU-8806, Done). Now applying feedback to Learning Hub Phase 2 (GDCU-8951, In Progress) while overseeing local navigation for news site articles moving into QA (GDCU-8957, In QA). Four Jira tickets active or completed yesterday — strong delivery day.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: Completed a refactor of Vue component styles and progressed Core Web Vitals performance improvements into QA. Also finalized the revenue share operator list feature for VIP users, which has transitioned to the product phase. Note: Jira shows related tickets (GDCU-8894 Vue refactor, GDCU-8925 CWV improvements) assigned to Novica Vukobratovic, suggesting close collaboration on these shared technical tasks.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: Successfully completed two tasks: reduced HTML payload sizes across all markets to address page bloat (GDCU-8874, Done) and implemented UX fixes for Cool Tones Phase 2 (GDCU-8955, Done). Both finalized, showing a priority on technical SEO and design consistency. Both confirmed Done in Jira.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: Focused on implementing the Oplist redesign for the Online Casinos page across Canadian and French markets (GDCU-8878, In Progress). Development is ongoing with no immediate blockers or additional priorities reported. This is a high-priority ticket flagged in delivery cycle planning.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. No Slack messages or Jira updates observed.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: The revenue share operator list feature for VIP users (GDCU-8847) has moved to "In Product" status, indicating it is being finalized for live rollout. This feature targets users identified as VIPs within the ktag system — a key Company OKR item.

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: Refactored scoped styles on Vue components (GDCU-8894, Done) and completed Core Web Vitals performance improvements based on AI recommendations (GDCU-8925, now In QA). Also reached out in #gdc-pod on 11 May enquiring about optinmonster campaign management, indicating broader cross-team coordination. Two strong Jira completions alongside Slack activity.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: Completed research on blocking restricted VPN users from free-to-play games (GDCU-8899, Done) and resolved duplicate payment methods appearing on Icelandic and Belgian brand reviews (GDCU-8959, Done). Has flagged a new technical blocker: Error 431 when attempting to access the GM Publish Error Scan on Jenkins — this requires immediate follow-up as it is blocking the publish error monitoring workflow.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: Focused on establishing the test automation framework and setup plan for Gambling.com to streamline QA processes. Also documented performance insights and coverage data for the site's Top Sites pages to better understand content reach. Balancing technical infrastructure setup with product performance analysis.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: Focused on establishing the test automation framework for Gambling.com while refining user account profile documentation. Prioritizing authentication flow automation for sign-up and sign-in. Recent Slack update (8 May, #gdc-pod) confirmed closed tickets for Ace navigation, VIP users, and Cool Tones, plus ongoing IUJ automation work — consistent with the Viktor digest summary.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. No Slack messages or Jira updates observed.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. No Slack messages or Jira updates observed.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. No Slack messages or Jira updates observed.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: Successfully oversaw the completion of the in-app age gate implementation for 18+ verification requirements — the ticket (GDCU-8806) is now marked Done, developed by Carlos Blauth with Gabriel as the product owner driving compliance. No additional blockers or pending items reported in recent activity.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: Active in content and SEO management: ordered missing brand reviews, bulk-updated slot pages to noindex/nofollow, created a new Melodifestivalen page, and reviewed UX designs for slots charts. Finalized weekly performance reports for non-English markets. Currently preparing a significant backlog of brand reviews for the IS/EN regions. Diana posted in #gdc-nonen-sync (1 May) referencing card carousel documentation, consistent with ongoing non-English market coordination.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: No activity in the last 24 hours. Most recent Slack activity was in #core-division-leads around 5–7 May coordinating the Matrix API requests list and weekly division reports. No messages from 11 May found.
